title: Quick Sort
description: Implement the quick_sort algorithm to sort an array of integers. The quick_sort algorithm uses a divide-and-conquer strategy to efficiently sort elements. Choose a pivot element from the array, partition the other elements into two sub-arrays according to whether they are less than or greater than the pivot, and recursively apply the same logic to the sub-arrays.

examples.explanation: In this example, the quick_sort algorithm picks a pivot (e.g., 6) and partitions the array into elements less than 6 and those greater than 6, resulting in smaller sub-arrays. After recursively sorting these sub-arrays, we achieve a fully sorted array.
Here, selecting 8 as a pivot divides the array into [7, 1, 5] and [10, 9]. Sorting each sub-array recursively leads us to the final sorted array.
Since the array is already sorted, the quick_sort algorithm will recognize that no swaps are needed, maintaining the original order throughout the recursive calls.

constraints: The input array can contain negative numbers.
The size of the input array is between 1 and 10^4.
hints: Consider choosing different pivot strategies to optimize performance.
Think about how you can partition the array around the pivot effectively.
Be mindful of cases with duplicate elements and how they affect sorting.
Explore iterative implementations to avoid recursion depth issues.
